data.body:
R2/b080 (operator-attested evidence-kind DET lint) — DESIGN grounded in code:
1) DET floor checks (P1-P9) are HARDCODED in det_floor.py DET_CHECKS + registry.py CANONICAL_DET; they are NOT routed via criteria_routing.json. validate_packaged_routing() (registry.py:445-446) FLAGS any routing key not in CANONICAL_LLM as an ORPHAN -> adding a DET entry to packaged criteria_routing.json would BREAK CI. So: NO criteria_routing.json change (task's suggestion refuted by code, per G1G2/E4).
2) Existing test test_det_invariants.py:327 asserts DET_CHECKS stays exactly P1-P9 ('the lint EXTENDS p6, must not add a P10'). Precedent: the verify-command lint (G-3a) was folded INTO p6_ac_quality. So this lint EXTENDS p6_ac_quality (advisory; p6 never blocks) via a pure helper _operator_evidence_ac_gaps(); no P10, DET_CHECKS stays 9.
3) The [operator-attested] tag matcher is workflow_ops._OPERATOR_ATTESTED_AC_RE (workflow_ops.py:61). det_floor imports nothing from workflow_ops and workflow_ops imports det_floor lazily -> det_floor will OWN _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE (single source) and workflow_ops imports it (no cycle) so lint + completion-verifier agree.
4) Self-gate: DETERMINISTIC lexicon eval over the 556 outcome-corpus tickets (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl -> 3091 AC items, 31 tagged). Final principled lexicon: flag rate 2.07% (<=5%), recall 61% (19/31), precision ~92% on a 64-item census (6 clear FPs), 115b + 8c4f both fire. NO LLM in the eval.
5) Landing: 2-commit stack for git-ancestry ('eval committed BEFORE lint lands'): commit1 = frozen corpus + harness + eval.json (uses the real dormant helper); commit2 = wire helper into p6 + tests + docs.
